channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Military Campaign to Wind Down and Plans National Address
item.description: “We will be leaving very soon,” the president told reporters in the Oval Office. A few hours later, the White House said Mr. Trump would provide “an important update on Iran” on Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
item.description: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne, Valerie Hopkins and Oleg Matsnev
item.category: Roskomnadzor (Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology and Mass Media) (Russia)
item.media:description: Of all the examples of growing repression in Russia during four years of war in Ukraine, few have touched more people than deepening internet restrictions.

item.title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
item.description: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Amanda Taub
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A weapons factory in Herstal, Belgium, this year. Most European countries have realized that they need to spend a lot more on guns to reduce their military dependence on the United States.

item.title: King Charles to Visit U.S. as Tensions Rise Between Trump and Britain
item.description: President Trump has fired criticism at America’s longtime ally over the war in Iran, but there are hopes that the royal visit will help shore up relations.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla hosted President Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, for a state banquet at Windsor Castle last year.

item.title: First Canadian Astronaut Will Travel to the Moon Amid Fraying U.S.-Canada Relations
item.description: Canada will send its first astronaut to the moon on a joint mission with the United States, but back on Earth, the relationship between the two countries is fraying.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
item.media:description: Robert Lucas of MDA Space helped develop the Canadarm3, a robotic system that will support astronauts during spacewalks and aid repairs in orbit.

item.title: U.S. Assures Colombian President Petro He Does Not Face Charges Right Now
item.description: The Justice Department is examining Gustavo Petro for possible ties to drug traffickers. U.S. officials have told him that for now he does not face criminal charges arising from the probes.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Gustavo Petro of Colombia at the presidential palace in Bogotá in March.

item.title: These Two Countries With Social Media Bans Aren’t Happy With Google and Meta
item.description: Indonesia says the two companies are violating its law that bars children under 16 from the social media. Australia has similar concerns.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Laura Chung
item.media:description: Earlier this month, the Indonesian government announced that social media companies must deactivate accounts of users under 16 years of age and put age-verification systems in place.

item.title: U.S. Senators Press Taiwan to Raise Military Spending, as China Protests
item.description: Four visiting senators urged Taiwan to break an impasse over a $40 billion budget proposal, highlighting concerns in Washington about the threat from China.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:credit: Taiwan Presidential Office, via Associated Press

item.title: How the U.K.’s Leader Decides Which U.S. Bombers to Let Fly Against Iran
item.description: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman, Eric Schmitt and Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at a meeting in London on Monday. “We will not be drawn into the wider war,” he told lawmakers in mid-March.

item.title: A Cat-and-Mouse Game of Russian Internet Restrictions and Evasion
item.description: As the Kremlin spends heavily on censorship technology, Russians are scrambling to find new ways to circumvent the limits.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins, Paul Sonne and Oleg Matsnev
item.category: Roskomnadzor (Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology and Mass Media) (Russia)
item.media:description: With the authorities blocking apps and websites, Russians are trying to find workarounds.

item.title: When Will Japan’s Cherry Blossoms Bloom? A.I. Can Help Answer That
item.description: Experts use artificial intelligence to analyze data, plus thousands of crowdsourced photos, to forecast the prized flowers, which are a multibillion-dollar attraction.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Kiuko Notoya, Hisako Ueno and Kentaro Takahashi
item.media:description: Visitors gathered beneath cherry blossoms at Ueno Park in Tokyo last week.

item.title: He Led Congo for 18 Years. Now, Joseph Kabila Is a Hunted Man.
item.description: Joseph Kabila, the former president, faces the death penalty after the government convicted him of treason last year. He says the charges are bogus.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean
item.media:credit: Guerchom Ndebo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Joseph Kabila returned to the Democratic Republic of Congo last year, to territory controlled by rebels.

item.title: Israel Passes Law to Hang Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
item.description: Experts say the law was written in such a way as to ensure that it was unlikely to ever apply to Jewish extremists who commit similar crimes.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Johnatan Reiss
item.media:description: Itamar Ben-Gvir, center, the national security minister, and other Israeli hard-liners had campaigned on a promise to execute Palestinian militants.

item.title: Where Million-Dollar Coastal Homes Glitter Near Metal Shacks
item.description: A shortage of affordable housing in the coastal city in South Africa has forced many people to live far outside the city center, while tourists occupy prime real estate.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Joao Silva

item.title: The Sailors Stranded in the Persian Gulf
item.description: Thousands of civilian sailors have been stranded for more than a month in waters surrounded by a conflict zone because of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Almost 20,000 seafarers are stranded in the Persian Gulf, according to the International Maritime Organization, as Iran has effectively blocked the exit through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: In UK Prisons, Drones Fly In Contraband ‘As if by Uber Eats’
item.description: Decades-old prison buildings were designed to be secure from the ground but not the air. Experts say that makes a lucrative smuggling trade hard to tackle.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden

item.title: Cape Town’s Housing Problem
item.description: The rise of tourist rentals in Cape Town is driving up housing costs, deepening the inequality in the South African city that’s a legacy of apartheid. Our reporter John Eligon explores a multihour commute taken by workers who are priced out of the city.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Joao Silva, Christina Thornell, Jon Miller, Leila Medina and June Kim

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Be Out of Iran Within Two to Three Weeks
item.description: The White House said the president would address the nation about Iran on Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump told reporters on Tuesday that he had attained his goal of dealing with Iran’s nuclear program and that gasoline prices in the United States would be coming down.

item.title: Israel Strikes Tehran Pharmaceutical Plant, Claiming Military Link
item.description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said on Tuesday that Israel was “openly and unashamedly bombing pharmaceutical companies.”
item.dc:creator: Parin Behrooz and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damaged apartments in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Narges Mohammadi Denied Treatment after Heart Attack in Iranian Prison, Husband Says
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Fredrik Varfjell/NTB Scanpix, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Ali and Kiana Rahmani received the Nobel Peace Prize in Oslo on behalf of their mother, Narges Mohammad, in 2023.

item.title: South Africa Sees Maritime Traffic Surge Amid Iran War
item.description: Global shipping companies looking for safer routes are turning to the Cape of Good Hope, a much longer, more expensive journey.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo vessels moored at the harbor in Cape Town in South Africa.

item.title: Forgoing Oversight of Iran War, G.O.P. Calls Hegseth to Testify on Budget
item.description: After resisting calls for public hearings for weeks, House Republicans have called the secretary of defense to testify at a budget hearing in late April for the first time since the attacks on Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at a news conference at the Pentagon in Arlington, Va., on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, took questions from reporters for the first time in nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.N. Peacekeepers in Lebanon Were Killed by Roadside Bomb
item.description: Members of the United Nations Security Council condemned deadly attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in south Lebanon and called for de-escalation between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.media:description: U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon earlier this month.

item.title: Is Social Media the New Tobacco?
item.description: A landmark court ruling in an online addiction case could be very bad news for big tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

item.title: Confident of Air Supremacy, U.S. Sends B-52 Bombers Over Iran
item.description: Iran continued to retaliate across the region on Tuesday but markets saw hopes that fighting might ebb. Israel said it would occupy a large chunk of Lebanon even after the war ends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, John Ismay, Helene Cooper and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth speaking during a press briefing at the Pentagon in Arlington, Va., on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Seeks to Redefine ‘Regime Change’ in Iran War
item.description: President Trump and his aides have made contradictory statements on whether the United States and Israel have transformed the Iranian government through violence.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said during his news conference on Tuesday that “this new regime, because regime change has occurred, should be wiser than the last.”

item.title: Trump Shares Video of Bombing in Iranian City of Isfahan
item.description: President Trump shared a video of the attack, which the Pentagon said had targeted an ammunition depot.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Why Australia Enshrined ‘a Succulent Chinese Meal’ Rant in Its Archive
item.description: A clip of the con man Jack Karlson delivering a theatrical soliloquy while being arrested in 1991 has been deemed one of the country’s important cultural recordings.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe

item.title: US Journalist Shelly Kittleson Was Abducted in Iraq, Officials Say
item.description: The freelance journalist was identified as Shelly Kittleson, who has worked for various news organizations, including Al-Monitor, which called for her safe and immediate release.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Falih Hassan and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel Signals Plans to Occupy Southern Lebanon After Ground Invasion
item.description: Israeli forces will control “the entire area” from the border to the Litani River, up to 20 miles into Lebanon, the defense minister said. He indicated Israel would control it after the ground invasion ends.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:description: A commercial district in Tyre, Lebanon, heavily damaged by Israeli strikes on Friday.

item.title: From Oil to Food, the Iran War Is Squeezing the Global Economy
item.description: The effects of the war in Iran are squeezing consumers, businesses and governments around the world, raising the prices of many essential goods.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Taiwo Aina for The New York Times
item.media:description: A fertilizer plant outside Lagos, Nigeria. Fertilizer shortages driven by the fighting in the Middle East, where crucial inputs are produced, can lead to poorer harvests and higher food prices around the world.

item.title: Trump Lashes Out at Europe Over Strait of Hormuz, Tells U.K. to ‘Go Get Your Own Oil’
item.description: President Trump’s latest outbursts followed reports that European countries were imposing more restrictions on American aircraft in their airspace.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Catherine Porter
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump arriving in Washington on Sunday. Mr. Trump criticized European allies on social media on Tuesday for their refusal to get more involved in the Iran War.

item.title: Arab World Faces ‘Profound’ Economic Crisis From Iran War, U.N. Agency Warns
item.description: An economic simulation warned that the region’s economy could lose more than $190 billion in just one month, and that Gulf states that have often bankrolled reconstruction efforts will be less able to help.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Unloading relief supplies at an aid distribution site in Tyre, Lebanon, last week.

item.title: International Booker Prize Shortlist: 6 Novels With ‘Burning Humanity’
item.description: Books by Marie NDiaye, Daniel Kehlmann and Rene Karabash are among the shortlisted titles for the major award for fiction translated into English.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall

item.title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
item.description: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and his tight circle of close aides have made opening the Strait of Hormuz a new, and nonnegotiable, issue.

item.title: Inside Russia During an Internet Crackdown
item.description: Our international correspondent Valerie Hopkins walks us through how she connects to the internet in Russia as the Kremlin clamps down and Moscow experiences major internet blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins, Rebecca Suner and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Edward Wong, Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Ephrat Livni

item.title: Kuwaiti Oil Tanker Set Ablaze After Attack Off the Coast of Dubai
item.description: The attack damaged the hull, the tanker's owner said. No oil spill was detected, according to the authorities in the United Arab Emirates.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Dubai port in the United Arab Emirates in 2022.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: Iran maintained that no negotiations have been held with the United States, and said none would while the fighting continues.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents viewing the damage to their apartment in Tehran after airstrikes on Monday.

item.title: Iran advances a plan to charge a toll for ships to traverse the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Callisto tanker anchored in Muscat, Oman, this month.

item.title: A New U.S. Missile Hit a Sports Hall in Iran. Here’s What We Know About It.
item.description: Barely out of prototype testing, the Precision Strike Missile is shrouded in secrecy — including which Persian Gulf countries the Army is launching them from.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Palm Sunday Attack in Nigeria Leaves at Least 12 Dead
item.description: No one has claimed responsibility for the killings in the mostly Christian city of Jos, and the police have yet to arrest the gunmen.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: A Nigerian military vehicle at the scene of an attack the morning after gunmen killed several people in Jos, Nigeria, on Monday.

item.title: Removing Unfriendly Leaders
item.description: The Trump administration appears to have embraced a new foreign policy tactic: getting rid of leaders it doesn’t like. Here’s how that can go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
item.description: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Adam Goldman and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Many senior Iranian leaders and their deputies have been killed, and those who survive have had difficulty communicating.

item.title: Trump Zigzags on Iran, Claiming ‘Great Progress’ but Making Threats
item.description: President Trump threatened to hit vital infrastructure in Iran if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened, but he also said that “regime change” had already been achieved.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Erika Solomon, Sanam Mahoozi and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump arriving at the White House Sunday night after returning from Florida.

item.title: Why Did the U.S. Allow a Russian Oil Tanker Through Its Cuba Blockade?
item.description: The ship’s arrival would bring Cuba some badly needed fuel. It also marks a shift from U.S. efforts to block oil shipments and pressure the Cuban government.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and David C. Adams
item.media:description: A satellite image taken last week of the port and oil terminal at the entrance of the Bay of Matanzas in northwestern Cuba.

item.title: As Trump Squeezes Cuba, U.S. Military Exists in a Bubble
item.description: The Navy base at Guantánamo Bay sparkles at night while neighbors across a Cuban minefield struggle in darkness driven by a Trump fuel embargo.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A bowling alley at the Guantánamo Bay military base on a Saturday. Life on the base has been mostly unaffected by the energy crisis in Cuba.

item.title: Iran Condemns Attacks on Its Universities, Warns of Retaliation
item.description: After attacks on several Iranian campuses, Iranian forces warn they could target American universities in the region.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Sanam Mahoozi and Sanjana Varghese
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Luis Carlos Rúa Fought Colombia’s Corruption in an Elephant Costume. Now He’s a Senator.
item.description: After years of anonymously documenting abandoned public works projects in Colombia, Luis Carlos Rúa revealed himself days before his election.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky and Esteban Vanegas
item.media:credit: Esteban Vanegas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Luis Carlos Rúa, in his white elephant character costume, at an abandoned government construction site in Pereira, Colombia.

item.title: Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse Artworks Are Stolen in 3-Minute Museum Heist, Police Say
item.description: Thieves broke into the Magnani-Rocca Foundation outside Parma, Italy, officials said, and made off with pieces worth millions.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins and Josephine de La Bruyère
item.media:description: “Les Poissons” by Pierre-Auguste Renoir was one of three paintings stolen this month from a museum in Italy.

item.title: Analysis shows where a new U.S. missile hit an Iranian sports hall and school.
item.dc:creator: Leanne Abraham and Christiaan Triebert

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Americans of Political Killings for Hire Overseas
item.description: Three men working for an American security contractor say the United Arab Emirates paid them to carry out targeted killings in Yemen. One of their targets is suing them in U.S. federal court.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:credit: Giles Clarke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Aden, Yemen, in 2018. It became the capital in exile for the internationally recognized Yemeni government, and was badly damaged by airstrikes in 2015.

item.title: Two More U.N. Peacekeepers Are Killed in Southern Lebanon
item.description: The deaths came a day after the U.N. secretary-general, António Guterres, condemned the killing of another peacekeeper.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon last week.

item.title: Trump Threatens to Destroy Kharg Island and Other Key Iranian Energy Sites
item.description: President Trump said in a newspaper interview that the United States might try to invade the Persian Gulf island, which is Iran’s main oil export hub.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The Port of Kharg Island Oil Terminal in Iran in 2017.

item.title: Israel Pulls Battalion From West Bank After Soldier Talks of ‘Revenge’
item.description: While detaining a CNN crew, a soldier said that Israeli troops were motivated by “revenge” against Palestinians and that all of the West Bank was “for the Jews.”
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Israeli soldiers in the occupied West Bank earlier this month.

item.title: Trump Claims ‘Regime Change’ in Iran Is Already Complete
item.description: President Trump suggested that Iran’s current leaders were “much more reasonable” as he sought to show progress in his war aims. Iran’s power structure appears firmly in control despite a month of U.S.-Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking aboard Air Force One, in Florida, on Sunday.

item.title: Xi Invites Taiwan’s Opposition Leader to Talk ‘Peace’ Ahead of Trump Summit
item.description: The planned visit by Cheng Li-wun appears designed to show Beijing’s influence and convey a benign message ahead of the summit with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Amy Chang Chien
item.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cheng Li-wun, leader of the Nationalist Party, at her office in Taipei, Taiwan, in November.

item.title: Australian Police Kill Man Suspected of Slaying 2 Officers
item.description: The fugitive, who was trained in surviving in the bush, had been on the run since a deadly confrontation last August with officers who had come to execute a search warrant.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.media:description: A funeral service for one of the slain police officers was held last year in Melbourne.

item.title: Travel Influencers Share Destinations So Good That They Returned
item.description: Even travel influencers, who spend their lives on the go, have their hearts stolen by certain places.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Gomelsky
